summary: Russian air strikes using FAB-3000 bombs hit Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic. Russian FAB-250 bombs struck Sloviansk, injuring at least five civilians including a child. Ukrainian forces attacked the "Krymsky Titan" plant in Armiansk, causing a fire and halting production.
key_points: Russian pilots destroyed Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic with FAB-3000 aerial bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Three FAB-250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, injuring at least five people, including a 9‑year‑old child (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Ukrainian forces struck the "Krymsky Titan" plant in Armiansk, igniting a fire and halting production of titanium, rocket fuel, gunpowder and explosives (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Russian defence ministry reported 249 Ukrainian UAVs were downed over Russian territory overnight (Source: @tass_agency/380561).
Multiple UAV groups were observed moving toward frontline areas in Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/65132, @kpszsu/65128, @kpszsu/65114, @kpszsu/65141).
Ukrainian special forces destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, located near civilian buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on eastern and north‑eastern directions, raising threat levels (Source: @kpszsu/65147).

sections.executive_summary: Russian air strikes using FAB-3000 bombs hit Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic. Russian FAB-250 bombs struck Sloviansk, injuring at least five civilians including a child. Ukrainian forces attacked the "Krymsky Titan" plant in Armiansk, causing a fire and halting production.

points: Ukrainian forces hit the "Krymsky Titan" plant in Armiansk, a facility producing titanium products, rocket fuel, gunpowder and explosives, igniting a fire and suspending output (Source: @UkraineNow/81896).
Special forces destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, located near a café, a children's goods store and other civilian structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
Multiple UAV groups were reported moving from Kharkiv's Loziv district toward Pavlograd in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating reconnaissance or strike preparation (Source: @kpszsu/65132).
UAV activity was noted from Zaporizhzhia toward the city from the southwest, suggesting potential targeting of logistics hubs (Source: @kpszsu/65126).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading from Sumy northward and from Chernihiv toward Desna/Oster from the south, indicating pressure on northern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/65114).
Russian pilots destroyed Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic using FAB-3000 aerial bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Three FAB-250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, causing injuries to at least five civilians, including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Russian air defence reported downing 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
The Russian defence ministry announced 249 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory overnight (Source: @tass_agency/380561).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on eastern and north‑eastern fronts, raising the threat of air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/65147).
Ukrainian UAV groups moved from Kharkiv's Loziv district toward Pavlograd in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating activity on the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65132).
Russian FAB-250 bombings hit Sloviansk in the east, injuring civilians and damaging over 20 multi‑apartment buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81898).
Ukrainian special forces destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, a city on the eastern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
Russian tactical aviation was noted as active on the eastern and south‑eastern directions, heightening risk for Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/65147).
UAV activity reported from Zaporizhzhia toward the north‑east of Kharkiv and Donetsk, suggesting pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/65118).
UAVs were observed on the Chernihiv front moving toward Desna/Oster from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65114).
UAVs were reported on the Chernihiv front heading toward Mena/Sosnytsia from the north (Source: @kpszsu/65138).
UAVs were seen near the border of Sumy and Kharkiv heading toward Poltava, indicating north‑eastern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65115).
Russian air defence claimed to have downed 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions, reflecting heightened aerial activity near the northern border (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Russian tactical aviation activity was flagged on the north‑eastern direction, increasing threat to northern Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/65144).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from Zaporizhzhia toward the south‑east, potentially targeting Russian‑held areas in the south (Source: @kpszsu/65128).
UAV activity was noted from the Kherson direction toward the Black Sea, aligning with Russian reports of UAV interceptions over the sea (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Russian defence ministry announced 249 Ukrainian UAVs were downed over Russian territory overnight (Source: @tass_agency/380561).
Russian tactical aviation activity was observed on eastern, north‑eastern and north‑eastern fronts, indicating potential strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/65147, @kpszsu/65144).
Ukrainian forces employed FAB-3000 bombs against Russian‑held points in the Donetsk People's Republic, showing escalation in aerial strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Ukrainian UAV groups operated across multiple fronts, including Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv, reflecting extensive drone deployment (Source: various @kpszsu posts).

key_developments: Russian pilots used FAB-3000 aerial bombs to destroy Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People's Republic, indicating a high‑payload strike capability. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875)
Three FAB-250 bombs were dropped on Sloviansk, injuring at least five civilians, including a child, and damaging over 20 residential buildings. (Source: @UkraineNow/81898)
Ukrainian forces struck the "Krymsky Titan" plant in Armiansk, igniting a fire and halting production of titanium, rocket fuel, gunpowder and explosives. (Source: @UkraineNow/81896)
Russian air defence reported downing 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea in a single day, showing intensified air‑defence activity. (Source: @tass_agency/380533)
The Russian defence ministry announced 249 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory overnight, reflecting a surge in UAV operations. (Source: @tass_agency/380561)
Ukrainian special forces destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, located near civilian infrastructure, reducing enemy drone capability. (Source: @UkraineNow/81893)
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on eastern and north‑eastern fronts, raising the threat of air strikes against Ukrainian positions. (Source: @kpszsu/65147)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian high‑payload bombing raids on Ukrainian logistical points in the Donetsk region and the use of FAB-250 bombs in Sloviansk demonstrate an escalation in strike intensity. Ukrainian UAV deployments across multiple fronts increase pressure on Russian supply lines but face growing Russian air‑defence effectiveness, as evidenced by large numbers of downed drones. The continued activity of Russian tactical aviation on eastern and north‑eastern axes poses a heightened threat to Ukrainian ground forces and critical infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to be intensifying drone and precision strike operations, targeting Russian logistics hubs such as the "Krymsky Titan" plant and UAV launch sites in Pokrovsk. Russian forces are responding with robust air‑defence measures, claiming high UAV kill counts, and increasing tactical aviation sorties to contest Ukrainian air activity. Both sides are concentrating efforts on the eastern and northern fronts, where most of the reported engagements occur.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Russian FAB‑3000 and FAB‑250 bomb deployments should be prioritized to assess future high‑payload strike patterns. UAV movement reports from Kharkiv, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv indicate coordinated drone corridors that may be exploited for targeting logistics. Russian air‑defence claims of large UAV losses suggest possible over‑reporting; independent verification is needed. Continued observation of tactical aviation activity on eastern and north‑eastern fronts will inform threat forecasts for upcoming operations.
